news.headline: NeurAxis Declares Q2 2026 Stock Dividend on Series B Preferred Stock
news.summary: The board declared a Q2 2026 stock dividend on Series B Preferred payable in ~80,463 common shares on July 29 (record date July 21). Routine non-cash preferred housekeeping that adds a modest ongoing dilution drip.

news.headline: NRXS Named Among 22 Biotech Stocks to Watch
news.summary: A TheStreet Pro screen of 22 biotech/medtech names to watch featured NRXS, adding incremental retail visibility for the thinly traded micro-cap. Media commentary rather than a company event.

news.headline: NeurAxis, Inc. added to Russell 3000E Growth Benchmark
news.summary: NRXS was added to the Russell 3000E Growth benchmark in the annual reconstitution, broadening passive-fund ownership and index visibility; the stock marked a 52-week high of $9.33 on June 27.

news.headline: NeurAxis Stockholders Approve Directors and Equity Compensation Plans
news.summary: At the June 10 annual meeting shareholders elected six directors and approved an increase to the equity incentive plan and a new ESPP. A governance milestone relevant mainly for additional potential stock-comp dilution.

news.headline: NRXS: Record Q1 revenue and expanded coverage drive growth in pediatric and VA neuromodulation markets
news.summary: Analyst update citing record Q1 revenue and growing insurance coverage, with the Veterans Affairs market flagged as a growth area alongside pediatrics. Backs the bullish view following the Category I CPT code launch, with Q1 revenue of $1.61M (+80% YoY) and ~86% gross margin.

news.headline: NeurAxis Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Financial Results Driven by 80% Year Over Year Revenue Growth
news.summary: Q1 revenue rose 80% YoY to $1.6M at 86.4% gross margin with a 24% narrower operating loss, driven by higher IB-Stim unit deliveries and pricing following the new Category I CPT code. Management guided to burn of $1M or less for the rest of 2026 and framed ~$15M revenue as the path to cash-flow breakeven.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report (expected mid-August) will show whether the Category I CPT-code tailwind is sustaining 70-80%+ revenue growth and continued burn reduction.

catalysts.label: Commercial payer coverage expansion
catalysts.detail: Additional positive medical policies from major commercial insurers would directly increase covered lives and reimbursed IB-Stim volume; management's highest strategic priority.

catalysts.label: Path to ~$15M revenue / cash-flow breakeven
catalysts.detail: Management targets roughly $15M annual revenue for cash-flow breakeven with burn guided to $1M or less for the rest of 2026, reducing near-term dilution risk.

catalysts.label: Series B stock dividend payment
catalysts.detail: Declared Q2 preferred stock dividend of ~80,463 common shares pays July 29 to holders of record July 21.

pulse: NeurAxis is executing on the January 2026 Category I CPT code catalyst, posting 80% YoY Q1 revenue growth, 86%+ gross margins and narrowing losses while guiding to sharply lower cash burn and a ~$15M breakeven target. Sentiment is constructive: Russell 3000E Growth inclusion, a 52-week high of $9.33 in late June, and steady payer-coverage wins support the reimbursement-driven thesis. Early-July flow was largely housekeeping (routine Series B stock dividend, a sector-watch mention), signaling no operational change but a small dilution drip. The next proof point is the mid-August Q2 print; main risks remain the going-concern note, ATM dilution, and micro-cap volatility.
